review_notes: The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security controls related to access enforcement and protection of user and system information. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but there is room for slight improvements in clarity and consistency. The functions are technically correct and align with the NIST AC-3(6) control, even though it is withdrawn, as they focus on access enforcement and protection of information.
title: Access Enforcement - Access enforcement | protection of user and system information [withdrawn
step1_reasoning: The control AC-3(6) focuses on access enforcement and protection of user and system information. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that RBAC policies do not grant excessive privileges (e.g., avoiding cluster-admin bindings) and enforcing least privilege principles. Additionally, network policies can be used to restrict ingress and egress traffic to protect system information from unauthorized access, similar to cloud network security configurations.
